evidence_quotes.quote: deliberate removal or destruction of video games

paragraphs.text: I would like to suggest the addition of a protection against the deliberate removal or destruction of video games as part of this initiative. In recent years, game publishers have become increasingly comfortable with the practice of revoking access to video games that customers have already purchased, often when the games are deemed no longer profitable or, in some cases, for arbitrary or undisclosed reasons. The root of the issue often lies in an over-reliance on online functionalities, such as intrusive battle passes or microtransaction stores, even in games that are otherwise purely single-player. As a result, many titles have already been removed and remain inaccessible to the customers who paid for them, with many more at risk if the trend continues unchecked. I believe this issue aligns with the goals of the Digital Fairness Act and urge you to consider its inclusion. Thank you for your time and consideration.
